denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/19/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102581594          Group #: GRP-1326
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-148683
Claim #: CLM-2026-205279
Date of Service (requested): 03/30/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 04/01/2026 for Pessary fitting plus surgical consult related to Uterine prolapse, symptomatic (N81.2). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Pessary fitting plus surgical consult
Diagnosis (as submitted): Uterine prolapse, symptomatic (N81.2)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied:
Guideline applied: pediatric imaging criteria (ages 0–17) referenced for this request (incorrect demographic guideline). Coverage Policy 106 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (CPG) and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 106 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Uterine prolapse, symptomatic (N81.2); Pessary fitting plus surgical consult exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

The file does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. This review used clinical criteria from our designated utilization-management program; the criteria document is available upon request.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102581594) and reference UM-2026-148683. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Uterine prolapse, symptomatic (N81.2). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Pessary fitting plus surgical consult recommended as the next appropriate step. Stage II prolapse; bulge symptoms; failed kegel program. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Pessary fitting plus surgical consult in the setting of Uterine prolapse, symptomatic (N81.2); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
